page: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. Local storage consists of an 1181 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPUs included.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 35.47 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% tier, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieving 14.71 GB/sec. Multi-core stress-ng performance scores at 20081.26, and PassMark CPU score is 33317.5, both falling within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show 1477.57 GB/sec for small block cached reads, dropping to 141.96 GB/sec for large block uncached reads, and 58.49 GB/sec for large block writes. Database operations via PassMark score at 10336.74, while Redis SET operations sustain 34.81 million operations per second.

The E20ds_v7 offers high memory-to-vCPU density and a fast 4.2 GHz CPU clock speed, which provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallelization. However, the 10 physical core limit restricts multi-threaded scaling compared to larger instances. The inclusion of 1181 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability makes it suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications. It also supports basic machine learning tasks, with LLM inference speeds of 124.5 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 7B model.
description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It includes 1181 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 35.47 GB/sec, alongside average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth results. With an 8.0 GB memory-per-vCPU ratio, this instance provides qualitative resource density for memory-bound applications. It is suited for database operations, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring high memory capacity and fast single-core execution.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 1181 GB of local storage, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, 1181 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high memory density, and integrated local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache
1181 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance of 35.47 GB/sec
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
